questions.q: In which Indian state is Bekal Fort located?
questions.explanation: Bekal Fort stands on the coast of the Kasaragod district in the northern part of Kerala.

questions.q: Bekal Fort overlooks which body of water?
questions.explanation: The fort sits on a headland projecting into the Arabian Sea along Kerala's western coast.

questions.q: What distinctive shape is Bekal Fort famous for?
questions.explanation: Bekal Fort is renowned for its large keyhole-shaped layout when viewed from above.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of Bekal Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort is built largely from laterite, a reddish stone abundant in coastal Kerala.

questions.q: Bekal Fort is located in which district of Kerala?
questions.explanation: Bekal Fort lies in the Kasaragod district, the northernmost district of Kerala.

questions.q: What is the most prominent surviving structure inside Bekal Fort?
questions.explanation: A large circular observation tower is the fort's most prominent feature, offering sweeping views of the sea.

questions.q: Bekal Fort is best described as which type of fort?
questions.explanation: Bekal is a coastal fort built on a rocky headland to guard the Arabian Sea shoreline.

questions.q: Bekal Fort is considered one of the largest forts in which region of India?
questions.explanation: Bekal is among the largest and best-preserved forts in Kerala.

questions.q: Which feature of Bekal Fort allowed defenders to spot approaching ships and threats?
questions.explanation: The observation tower gave a commanding lookout over the surrounding sea and land.

questions.q: What color is Bekal Fort's stonework, owing to its laterite construction?
questions.explanation: Laterite gives the fort its characteristic reddish-brown hue.

questions.q: Which ruler is most credited with building Bekal Fort in its present form in the 17th century?
questions.explanation: Bekal Fort is generally attributed to Shivappa Nayaka of the Keladi (Bednore) Nayaka dynasty around the mid-17th century.

questions.q: The Keladi Nayakas who built Bekal Fort were originally feudatories of which empire?
questions.explanation: The Keladi (Nayaka) rulers began as governors under the Vijayanagara Empire before becoming independent.

questions.q: In which century did Bekal Fort take its present form?
questions.explanation: The fort as it stands today was constructed in the mid-17th century by the Keladi Nayakas.

questions.q: After the Keladi Nayakas, which Mysore rulers gained control of Bekal Fort in the late 18th century?
questions.explanation: Bekal Fort came under the control of Hyder Ali and later his son Tipu Sultan of Mysore.

questions.q: After the defeat of Tipu Sultan in 1799, control of Bekal Fort passed to whom?
questions.explanation: Following Tipu Sultan's fall in 1799, the fort came under British East India Company control.

questions.q: A small mosque located near Bekal Fort is associated by tradition with which Mysore ruler?
questions.explanation: A mosque near the fort is traditionally linked to Tipu Sultan, who is said to have had it built during his time in the region.

questions.q: Which Hindu temple stands just outside the entrance of Bekal Fort?
questions.explanation: An old Anjaneya (Hanuman) temple stands near the fort's entrance, predating much of the present structure.

questions.q: Holes in the fort's outer walls served what defensive purpose?
options: Slots for cannon and musket fire
questions.explanation: The walls feature openings designed for firing cannons and muskets at attackers approaching by land or sea.

questions.q: Bekal Fort and its surrounding beach were popularized in Indian cinema by a famous song sequence from which 1990s Mani Ratnam film?
questions.explanation: The song 'Uyire' (Tu Hi Re) from the 1995 film Bombay was famously shot at Bekal Fort beach.

questions.q: Which government agency is primarily responsible for the protection of Bekal Fort as a monument of national importance?
questions.explanation: Bekal Fort is a centrally protected monument maintained by the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: Why was the observation tower at Bekal built with thick walls and a wide base?
options: To serve as a royal bedroom
To mount cannons and withstand artillery
questions.explanation: The robust, thick-walled tower was designed to mount artillery and serve as a strong defensive lookout.

questions.q: Which feature near Bekal Fort connected the interior to the sea, aiding escape or supply?
options: A tunnel/passage believed to lead toward the sea
questions.explanation: The fort is associated with a tunnel-like passage said to lead toward the sea, a common defensive feature.

questions.q: The name of the dynasty most associated with building Bekal Fort, the Keladi Nayakas, is also known by which alternative name?
questions.explanation: The Keladi Nayakas are also called the Ikkeri or Bednore Nayakas, after their later capitals.

questions.q: Before the Keladi Nayakas fortified Bekal, the area had been under the influence of which local Kerala dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Bekal region had been part of the domain of the Kolathiri (Chirakkal) rajas before the Nayaka conquest.

questions.q: Bekal Fort lies close to which small town that shares part of its name with the fort?
questions.explanation: The fort sits beside the village of Bekal, from which it takes its name, near Pallikkara in Kasaragod.

questions.q: Which feature of Bekal's design reflects its purpose as a purely military and strategic structure rather than a residential palace?
options: Absence of grand palatial residences within the walls
questions.explanation: Bekal lacks elaborate palaces inside, underscoring that it was built chiefly for defense and observation.

questions.q: Bekal Fort has been developed as a flagship project under which tourism initiative in Kerala?
questions.explanation: The Bekal Resorts Development Corporation (BRDC) was set up by the Kerala government to develop the fort and surrounding area as a tourism hub.

questions.q: What strategic advantage did the headland location of Bekal Fort provide to its rulers?
options: Command of coastal maritime trade and sea approaches
questions.explanation: Perched on a sea-facing headland, the fort commanded coastal trade routes and guarded against seaborne attack.

questions.q: Why is laterite considered a particularly suitable building material for a coastal fort like Bekal?
options: It is soft when quarried and hardens on exposure to air
questions.explanation: Laterite can be cut easily when freshly quarried and hardens durably on exposure to air, making it ideal for thick fort walls.

questions.q: The approximate area enclosed by Bekal Fort makes it notable for which reason among Kerala's forts?
options: It is the smallest fort in Kerala
It is one of the largest forts in Kerala by area
It is an entirely underwater fort
It is a fort made of timber
questions.explanation: Covering roughly 40 acres, Bekal is one of the largest forts in Kerala.
